description: Detection rules for suspicious process and service behavior

rules.name: Office Spawning Shell
rules.description: Office application spawning a command shell or scripting engine
rules.recommendation: Investigate the Office document and quarantine. Possible phishing or macro attack.

rules.name: PowerShell Spawning CMD
rules.description: PowerShell launching cmd.exe (common in multi-stage attacks)
rules.recommendation: Review PowerShell command history and script blocks for malicious activity.

rules.name: MSHTA Spawning Shell
rules.description: mshta.exe launching a shell process (used in LOLBin attacks)
rules.recommendation: Terminate mshta.exe and investigate HTA files.

rules.name: Explorer Spawning Script
rules.recommendation: Check recently opened files and shortcut (LNK) files.

rules.name: Suspicious Process from Temp
rules.description: Executable running from Windows Temp directory
rules.recommendation: Investigate the executable in the temp folder. Possibly a dropped payload.

rules.name: Process from Downloads
rules.description: Executable running directly from Downloads folder
rules.recommendation: Verify the file source. Users should not execute files directly from Downloads.

rules.name: Process from Recycle Bin
rules.description: Executable running from Recycle Bin (very suspicious)
rules.recommendation: Immediately terminate and quarantine. Malware hiding in Recycle Bin.

rules.name: Unsigned Process
rules.description: Process executable is not digitally signed
rules.recommendation: Verify the process identity and origin. Unsigned executables may indicate tampering.

rules.name: Service from Temp Folder
rules.description: Windows service binary located in a Temp folder
rules.recommendation: Stop and delete the service. Investigate the binary immediately.

rules.name: New Startup Entry
rules.description: New entry detected in startup locations
rules.recommendation: Review new startup entries. Could be persistence mechanism installed by malware.

rules.name: WMIC Spawning Process
rules.description: wmic.exe spawning child processes (used in lateral movement)
rules.recommendation: Review WMI subscriptions and WMIC command history.

rules.name: Certutil Downloading Files
rules.description: certutil.exe spawned by non-system process (often used to download payloads)
rules.recommendation: Check certutil usage and any downloaded files.

rules.name: Regsvr32 Abuse
rules.description: regsvr32.exe used as a LOLBin to bypass application whitelisting
rules.recommendation: Investigate regsvr32 command line arguments for scriptlet abuse.

rules.name: High CPU Usage Process
rules.description: Process consuming abnormally high CPU (possible cryptominer)
rules.recommendation: Investigate high CPU process. Could be crypto mining malware.

rules.name: Disabled Security Service
rules.recommendation: Re-enable security services immediately. System is potentially compromised.

rules.name: RunDLL32 Abuse
rules.description: rundll32.exe used to load potentially malicious DLL
rules.recommendation: Inspect rundll32 command line for suspicious DLL loading.

rules.name: Process from Public Folder
rules.recommendation: Investigate process in Public folder. Common lateral movement staging location.

rules.name: Script Running from AppData
rules.description: Script or executable running from AppData
rules.recommendation: Review process in AppData. Malware often hides here to evade detection.
